hints: The pattern r"ip=([\d.]+).*?status=(\d+)" works: [\d.]+ matches an IP address (digits and dots); .*? lazily skips the path field; \d+ matches the status code. Two capture groups means findall returns a list of (ip, status) tuples.
Loop over the tuples from findall: for ip, status in LOG_PATTERN.findall(log_text). Then check if status.startswith('4') to filter for 4xx codes.
To count occurrences, use counts[ip] = counts.get(ip, 0) + 1. counts.get(ip, 0) returns the current count (or 0 if the IP hasn't been seen yet), and you add 1 to it.
If 10.0.0.5 is showing up in your results, you're not filtering by status code. Only add an IP to counts when the status starts with '4'.
